rationale: Mathematics has the most stable subdiscipline structure of any field — the AMS subject classification (MSC) has been recognizable for decades. I followed the modern MSC top-level structure with two consolidations: I merged Combinatorics with Graph Theory (one identity), and kept Probability and Statistics separate (statistical methodology belongs in Statistics & Data Science at level 1, but probability-as-pure-math stays here). I added History & Philosophy of Mathematics as a small but real card — many pure mathematicians identify with foundations and history.

subspecialties.name: Algebra
covers: group theory, ring theory, representation theory, commutative algebra, homological algebra, algebraic K-theory

subspecialties.name: Algebraic Geometry
subspecialties.tagline: Schemes, moduli, derived categories, arithmetic geometry.
covers: algebraic geometry, schemes, moduli theory, derived algebraic geometry, arithmetic geometry, log geometry

subspecialties.name: Number Theory
covers: analytic number theory, algebraic number theory, L-functions, Langlands program, automorphic forms, modular forms

subspecialties.name: Analysis
covers: real analysis, complex analysis, functional analysis, harmonic analysis, operator theory, geometric measure theory

subspecialties.name: Partial Differential Equations
subspecialties.tagline: Nonlinear PDEs, dispersive, fluid PDEs, regularity.
covers: nonlinear PDEs, dispersive equations, geometric analysis of PDEs, Navier-Stokes regularity, kinetic theory

subspecialties.name: Geometry & Topology
subspecialties.tagline: Differential geometry, low-dim topology, geometric structures.
covers: differential geometry, Riemannian geometry, low-dimensional topology, knot theory, geometric topology, symplectic geometry

subspecialties.name: Combinatorics & Graph Theory
covers: enumerative combinatorics, extremal combinatorics, graph theory, algebraic combinatorics, additive combinatorics, design theory
explicitly_excludes: algorithms on graphs → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Logic & Foundations
subspecialties.tagline: Set theory, model theory, proof theory, category theory.
covers: mathematical logic, set theory, model theory, proof theory, computability theory, category theory foundations

subspecialties.name: Probability Theory
subspecialties.tagline: Stochastic processes, random structures, SDE, large deviations.
covers: stochastic processes, random matrices, random graphs, SPDEs, large deviations, percolation, Markov processes

subspecialties.name: Dynamical Systems & Ergodic Theory
subspecialties.tagline: Chaos, ergodic theory, hyperbolic dynamics, KAM.
covers: dynamical systems, ergodic theory, hyperbolic dynamics, KAM theory, complex dynamics, applications to number theory

subspecialties.name: Numerical Analysis & Scientific Computing
subspecialties.tagline: FEM, spectral methods, multigrid, numerical PDE.
covers: finite element methods, spectral methods, multigrid, numerical PDE, numerical linear algebra, uncertainty quantification (math angle)
explicitly_excludes: scientific computing software → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Optimization & Operations Research
covers: convex optimization, integer programming, stochastic optimization, operations research theory, game theory mathematics, network optimization
explicitly_excludes: ML optimization methods → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Mathematical Physics
subspecialties.tagline: QFT math, integrable systems, statistical mechanics math.
covers: mathematical aspects of QFT, integrable systems, mathematical statistical mechanics, string theory mathematics, gauge theory mathematics

subspecialties.name: Applied Math & Mathematical Biology
subspecialties.tagline: Math bio, math finance, math social science.
covers: mathematical biology, mathematical epidemiology, mathematical finance, control theory math, math social science, ABM mathematics

uncovered_concerns: Considered Computational Mathematics distinct from Numerical Analysis — kept merged; the working communities overlap heavily.
Considered Algebraic Topology as its own card — covered under Geometry & Topology and Algebraic Geometry.
Considered History/Philosophy of Mathematics — folded under Humanities at level 1.
